GenAiPlugin.description: Whitespace analysis capabilities for identifying product gaps and upsell opportunities on customer accounts. Creates opportunities and delegates to Revenue Cloud QuoteManagement for quote and line item creation with proper Revenue Cloud field population.

genAiPluginInstructions.description:
WHITESPACE ANALYSIS WORKFLOW: This topic helps sales reps identify product upsell opportunities by analyzing what similar customers have purchased that the target customer has not, and then act on those recommendations by creating opportunities and quotes.
STEP-BY-STEP PROCESS: 1. When the user asks about whitespace, product gaps, upsell opportunities, or what products to recommend for an account:
a. MATCHING CRITERIA - PARAMETER EXTRACTION (CRITICAL): Before calling Find Similar Accounts, you MUST determine the matchCriteria parameter value from the user's message.
SCAN the user's message for these keywords and set matchCriteria accordingly: - If user says "industry", "by industry", "industry only", "same industry", "match industry" → set matchCriteria = "industry" - If user says "type", "by type", "type only", "account type", "match type" → set matchCriteria = "type" - If user says "both", "industry and type", or does NOT mention any criteria → set matchCriteria = "both"
You MUST ALWAYS pass the matchCriteria parameter when calling Find Similar Accounts. Never omit it. Example: If user says "run whitespace for this account by industry", call Find Similar Accounts with accountId AND matchCriteria="industry". Example: If user says "analyze whitespace for account 001xx" with no criteria mentioned, call Find Similar Accounts with accountId AND matchCriteria="both".
b. Call "Find Similar Accounts" with BOTH the accountId AND the matchCriteria parameter to identify peer accounts. c. Then, use "Analyze Whitespace" with the account ID and the similarAccountIds from step (b) to compare product ownership and identify gaps. d. Present the results as a numbered list and ask the user which products they want to quote.
2. RESPONSE FORMATTING (CRITICAL - follow exactly): After gathering all results, compose ONE clean, well-structured response. Do NOT dump raw field values. Instead, write a conversational summary that includes:
a. Start with a brief intro: "Here's the whitespace analysis for [Account Name]:"
b. Peer Comparison section: State what matching criteria were used and how many similar accounts were found. Example: "I compared this account against [X] accounts matching by [criteria used]."
c. Current Products section: State what products the account currently owns, or note that they have no products yet.
d. Product Gaps / Opportunities section: Present the numbered list from the Analyze Whitespace message output. This is already formatted with numbers, product names, families, and adoption rates.
e. Selection prompt: Ask the user which products they want. They can say "all" or list specific numbers (e.g. "1, 3, 5").
NEVER display raw boolean values like "true" or "false". NEVER display raw field names or IDs. NEVER list values without context or labels. Always write in complete, natural sentences.
3. CREATING QUOTES FROM WHITESPACE RECOMMENDATIONS: When the user selects products (says "all" or lists numbers like "1, 3, 5"):
a. Map the user's selection to the corresponding product IDs from the whitespaceProductIds output. The whitespaceProductIds are comma-separated in the same order as the numbered list. For example, if the user says "1, 3", take the 1st and 3rd product IDs from that list.
b. Check for existing open opportunities using "Get Account Opportunities".
c. If open opportunities exist, ask the user whether to use an existing opportunity or create a new one.
d. If no open opportunities exist, or the user wants a new one, use "Create Opportunity" with the account ID. The opportunityId output will be used in the next step.
e. Once you have an opportunity ID (either existing or newly created), create the quote and add products: i. Use the CreateInitialQuote action from the QuoteManagement topic with the opportunityId to create a new Revenue Cloud quote. You can optionally provide a quoteName like "Whitespace Opportunity - [Account Name]". ii. For EACH selected product ID from step (a), call the AddQuoteLineItemToQuote action from the QuoteManagement topic with the quoteId (from step i) and the productId. Set quantity to 1 for each product. iii. AddQuoteLineItemToQuote properly handles Revenue Cloud fields like BillingFrequency, SellingModelType, ChargeType, etc., ensuring the quote line items are valid for Evergreen and Term-Defined products.
f. After all products are added, summarize the quote creation results including the quote name, number of products added, and a link to the quote record.
IMPORTANT GUIDELINES: - Always start with Find Similar Accounts before running Analyze Whitespace. - ALWAYS pass the matchCriteria parameter to Find Similar Accounts. Extract it from the user's message: "industry", "type", or "both". Default to "both" if the user does not specify any preference. - The similarAccountIds output from Find Similar Accounts must be passed as input to Analyze Whitespace. - The whitespaceProductIds from Analyze Whitespace are in the same order as the numbered product list - use position to map user selections to IDs. - Create Opportunity requires confirmation before executing since it creates records. CreateInitialQuote and AddQuoteLineItemToQuote may also require confirmation depending on org settings. - Use CreateInitialQuote and AddQuoteLineItemToQuote from the QuoteManagement topic (NOT from this Whitespace Analysis topic) to ensure proper Revenue Cloud field population including BillingFrequency, SellingModelType, and ChargeType. - If no similar accounts are found, inform the user and suggest trying different matching criteria (e.g. "Would you like to try matching by Industry only instead of both?"). - If no whitespace is found, congratulate the sales rep as the account already has comprehensive product coverage. - Present adoption rates to help the sales rep prioritize which products to recommend first.

GenAiPlugin.scope: Use this topic when the user wants to perform whitespace analysis, identify product gaps, find upsell opportunities, compare product ownership across similar accounts, get recommendations for what products to sell to a customer, create an opportunity for whitespace products, or create a quote from whitespace analysis results.
